topicCategories.title: Topic 1 运动与健身 Exercise & Fitness

questions.refText: Do you think exercising daily is important for good health? Why or why not?
Imagine you are going to recommend an exercise program for a friend who does not exercise at all. What kind of routine would you suggest and why?
What do you think might challenge your friend the most about sticking to this program? How would you help them?
Some people believe that having a reward after a good week of exercise is essential for staying motivated. Do you agree or disagree? Why?
Do you think it is better to exercise in the morning or in the evening? Why?
If you were designing a beginner workout plan for a family member, what three activities would you include? Why?
What do you think is the hardest habit to build when someone first starts exercising? How can they overcome it?
Some people believe that team sports are more beneficial than exercising alone because they also build social skills. What is your view?
Would you say physical fitness is as important as mental fitness? Can you explain your thinking?
Describe a type of exercise that you think almost anyone can do, regardless of age or fitness level. Why is it suitable for everyone?
What obstacles might prevent an elderly person from following a regular exercise routine? What solutions would you suggest?
Some experts argue that gyms are unnecessary because people can stay fit without them. Do you agree or disagree? Why?
How much exercise do you think a person needs each week to stay healthy? Why?
If a friend said they had no time to exercise, what practical advice would you give them to fit activity into their day?
What do you think is the biggest misconception people have about exercise? Why is it a problem?
Some people believe competitive sports put too much pressure on young people and should not be encouraged in schools. What do you think?
Do you think fitness apps and smartwatches have made it easier for people to exercise regularly? Why or why not?
Imagine you are helping a very busy coworker create a weekly fitness plan. What would you recommend?
What do you think motivates people more — wanting to look better or wanting to feel healthier? Why?
Some people say professional athletes are not good role models because they are paid to be fit. Do you agree with this view?
Is exercising outdoors better than exercising indoors, in your opinion? Why?
Think of a sport or physical activity popular in your country. Would you recommend it to a complete beginner? Why or why not?
What challenges might someone face when trying to exercise outdoors in a busy city? How could those be addressed?
Some people believe governments should invest more money in free public fitness facilities. Do you agree?
Do you think children today are less physically active than previous generations? Why do you think that is?
If you could design a school PE class that every student would enjoy, what activities would you include?
What factors might stop a teenager from joining regular physical activity? How could parents or teachers help?
Some people argue that physical education should be compulsory throughout all school years. What is your opinion?
Do you think diet or exercise is more important for maintaining a healthy weight? Why?
Imagine your younger sibling wants to start exercising but does not know where to begin. What first steps would you advise?
What mental health benefits do you think regular exercise provides? Can you give examples?
Some people believe that people who are overweight should pay more for health insurance because of lifestyle choices. Do you agree or disagree?
How do you think working from home has affected people's physical activity levels? Why?
If you could introduce one new exercise trend to your community, what would it be and how would you promote it?
What do you think is the most common reason people give up on an exercise routine after a few weeks?
Some people believe employers should give employees paid time during the workday to exercise. What do you think?
Do you think exercise is more of a personal responsibility or something society should actively encourage? Why?
Describe an ideal exercise routine for someone recovering from a mild illness. What would you include and why?
What do you think are the social benefits of exercising in a group compared to exercising alone?
Some people argue that extreme sports are too dangerous and should be regulated or even banned. What is your opinion?

topicCategories.title: Topic 2 健康与生活习惯 Health & Wellness

questions.refText: Do you have any specific daily routines or habits you use to maintain your physical well-being? If so, what are they?
Can you describe any dietary choices or habits that you or someone you know follows to stay healthy?
If you could make one significant change to your daily habits to improve your health, what would it be and why?
Some people believe that mental health is just as important as physical health and should receive equal attention. Do you agree? Why?
How important do you think sleep is for overall health? Do you think most people get enough sleep?
Describe the eating habits of someone in your family or a close friend. Would you say those habits are generally healthy?
What do you think is the most difficult healthy habit for most people to develop? What makes it so hard?
Some people argue that sugary drinks and fast food should be heavily taxed to discourage people from buying them. What do you think?
Do you think stress is one of the biggest threats to people's health today? Why or why not?
If a friend asked for advice on how to start eating more healthily, what practical tips would you give them?
What barriers might prevent someone on a low income from maintaining a healthy diet? What could help?
Some people believe workplaces should provide healthy meals for all employees. Do you agree?
Would you say that your city or community makes it easy or difficult to live a healthy lifestyle? Why?
Think of a wellness or health trend that has become popular recently. Would you recommend it? Why or why not?
What challenges do young people face when trying to adopt healthy habits? How could those be overcome?
Some people believe individuals are entirely responsible for their own health and governments should not interfere. What is your view?
Do you think people today are more health-conscious than they were 20 years ago? What evidence supports your view?
Describe a wellness practice — such as meditation, yoga, or a specific diet — that you think is particularly effective. Why?
What might prevent an older person from changing unhealthy habits they have had for many years?
Some people argue that health education should be a compulsory subject in all schools from an early age. Do you agree?
How much do you think social media influences people's ideas about health and body image? Is this positive or negative?
Imagine a friend is trying to reduce their screen time as part of a digital wellness routine. What steps would you suggest?
What do you think is the relationship between financial stress and physical health? Can you give examples?
Some people believe annual health check-ups should be free and mandatory for all adults. What do you think?
Do you think people rely too much on medication rather than making lifestyle changes to stay healthy? Why or why not?
Describe an ideal daily health routine for a university student. What would you include and why?
What obstacles might someone face when trying to quit an unhealthy habit, such as excessive social media use?
Some people believe doctors should spend more time advising patients on lifestyle changes rather than just prescribing medication. Do you agree?
In your opinion, is it possible to be healthy without following a strict diet or exercise plan? Why?
Think of someone you know who has a very healthy lifestyle. What habits do you think contribute most to their good health?
What do you think is the link between good mental health and good physical health? Which affects the other more?
Some people argue the healthcare system focuses too much on treating illness rather than preventing it. What is your opinion?
How important do you think hydration is for good health? Do you think people drink enough water daily?
If you were designing a wellness program for a company's employees, what elements would you include?
What challenges might arise when trying to maintain healthy habits during a very stressful or busy period?
Some people believe that people with unhealthy lifestyles should contribute more to healthcare costs. Do you agree or disagree?
Do you think the food available in your local area supports or hinders a healthy diet? Explain your view.
Describe a simple wellness habit that almost anyone can adopt, regardless of schedule or budget. Why would it help?
What do you think stops most people from making changes they know would improve their health?
Some people argue that food companies should be legally required to reduce the sugar and salt in their products. What do you think?

topicCategories.title: Topic 3 童年教育 Childhood Education

questions.refText: Did you enjoy your school experience as a child? What made it positive or negative?
Tell me about a school project or activity you participated in. What made that experience special or memorable?
Tell me about the technology you used during your childhood education. Do you feel it prepared you for life after school? Why or why not?
Some people believe the education system needs major changes to focus more on skills like problem-solving and teamwork rather than memorizing facts. Do you agree?
What subject did you enjoy the most at school and why? How has it influenced your life?
Think of a teacher who had a positive impact on you. What did they do that made such a difference?
What aspects of your childhood education do you think were lacking or could have been better?
Some people argue that children learn more from free play and exploration than from structured classroom lessons. Do you agree?
Were there extracurricular activities at your school? How important do you think these are for a child's development?
Describe a moment in your school life when you faced a real challenge. How did you deal with it?
In what ways do you think schools today are different from schools when you were a child?
Some people believe children should be grouped by ability in class rather than mixed together. What is your opinion?
Do you think homework is beneficial for children, or does it create unnecessary stress? Why?
Think about a school trip or outdoor learning experience you had. What did you gain from it?
What do you think is the most important skill schools should teach children to prepare them for adult life?
Some people argue that standardized tests are a fair and effective way to measure student ability. Do you agree?
How important was creativity in your school experience? Do you feel schools encourage creativity enough?
Describe the role that reading played in your education as a child. How has it shaped you?
What do you think are the main pressures children face at school today that perhaps did not exist in the past?
Some people believe arts subjects like music and drama should receive as much funding as science and maths. What do you think?
Did you go to a public or private school? Do you think there is a significant difference in the quality of education between them?
Think of a time when you worked on a group project at school. What did you learn from working with others?
What challenges do teachers face in today's classrooms that make their job more difficult?
Some people argue that parents have a greater influence on a child's education than teachers do. Do you agree?
Do you think children today are under more academic pressure than previous generations? Why or why not?
Describe a skill you wish your school had taught you but did not. Why do you think it was not included?
What do you think is the most effective way for a child to learn a second language at school?
Some people believe single-sex schools produce better academic results than mixed schools. What is your view?
How much do you think a child's home environment affects their performance at school?
Think of an activity outside of school — such as sport, music, or art — that contributed to your development. What did you gain?
What could schools do better to support students who are struggling academically?
Some people argue that university education should be free for all students, funded by the government. What is your opinion?
Was physical education an important part of your schooling? Why do you think PE is or is not valued enough in schools?
Describe how your school handled conflict or bullying among students. Was the approach effective?
What do you think are the advantages and disadvantages of online learning compared to traditional classroom teaching?
Some people believe schools should teach financial literacy as a compulsory subject. Do you agree?
How has your childhood education influenced the career path or life choices you have made?
Think of a moment at school when you helped a classmate or they helped you. What did you take away from that experience?
What do you think is the biggest challenge facing education systems around the world today?
Some people argue that technology in the classroom — such as tablets and laptops — does more harm than good for young learners. What do you think?

topicCategories.title: Topic 4 娱乐与电影 Entertainment & Movies

questions.refText: What kinds of movies do your family or friends generally like to watch? Why do you think they enjoy those genres?
When you watch a movie, do you prefer to watch on a weekday after school or work, or at the weekend? Why?
In the past, people mostly watched movies in cinemas. Today, many people watch at home. Do you think cinemas will continue to exist in the future? Why or why not?
Some people believe movies can be a powerful tool for educating people and raising awareness. Do you agree, or do you think there are more effective ways to educate people?
How often do you or your family watch films or TV shows? What platforms do you usually use?
Think of a film or TV series that had a strong impact on you or someone close to you. What made it so memorable?
What do you think has changed most about the way people consume entertainment over the past ten years?
Some people argue that streaming services like Netflix have had a negative impact on the film industry by undercutting cinemas. What is your view?
Do you prefer watching films alone or with other people? Why?
Describe a film or series you would recommend to someone who wants to improve their English. Why that one?
What challenges do independent or foreign-language filmmakers face when trying to reach a wide audience?
Some people believe films showing violence or crime should be banned because they encourage harmful behaviour. Do you agree?
Do you think animated films are only for children, or can they be meaningful for adults too? Why?
Think of a director or actor that your friends or family admire. What do you think makes their work stand out?
Why do you think certain genres — such as superhero films — remain so popular despite criticism from some critics?
Some people argue the film industry does not represent diverse cultures and backgrounds fairly. What is your opinion?
Would you say you watch more entertainment content now than you did a few years ago? What has changed?
Imagine a friend who has never watched a foreign-language film. Which one would you recommend and how would you convince them to watch it?
What do you think are the disadvantages of binge-watching TV series in terms of health or daily productivity?
Some people believe social media has made it impossible to avoid spoilers, ruining the experience of films. Do you agree?
Do you think live music concerts or performances are better entertainment than films? Why or why not?
Describe the entertainment preferences of someone in your household. How are they similar to or different from yours?
What do you think is lost when a novel is adapted into a film? Is anything gained?
Some people argue that the government should fund local film productions to preserve cultural identity. Do you agree?
Do you think comedy is the most universal film genre across different cultures? Why or why not?
Think of an advertisement or short film you found particularly creative. What made it stand out?
What factors do you think determine whether a film becomes a commercial success or fails at the box office?
Some people believe actors and directors earn far too much money compared to other professionals. What do you think?
How do you feel about watching films with subtitles? Do subtitles enhance or distract from the experience?
Describe how a person you know chooses what to watch. What criteria do they use?
What role do film festivals play in promoting new and diverse cinema? Are they still relevant today?
Some people argue that streaming platforms produce lower quality content because they prioritize quantity over quality. Do you agree?
Do you think documentaries are as entertaining as fiction films? Why or why not?
Think about a time a film or TV show changed your opinion about a social issue. What happened?
What do you think are the positive and negative effects of children spending a lot of time watching TV or online videos?
Some people believe film ratings and age restrictions are outdated in the streaming age. Do you agree?
How important is the social aspect of going to the cinema — such as going with friends — compared to the film itself?
Describe the type of entertainment content you think will be most popular ten years from now. Why?
What challenges does a filmmaker face when trying to appeal to both local and international audiences at the same time?
Some people argue that content created by artificial intelligence will eventually replace human filmmakers. What is your opinion?

topicCategories.title: Topic 5 兴趣爱好 Hobbies & Leisure

questions.refText: What kinds of hobbies do your friends or family have? Why do you think they enjoy them?
If your school or workplace organized after-work hobby clubs, which clubs do you think would interest the most people? Why?
Do you think hobbies should mostly be relaxing, or is it better if they challenge you? Why?
How important do you think it is for people to keep exploring new hobbies as they grow older? Why?
Do you have a hobby or interest that you regularly spend time doing? Tell me about it.
If you were to choose a new hobby, what would you choose and why?
What might prevent you from starting this new hobby or pastime?
Some people believe it is better to dedicate yourself fully to one hobby rather than pursue many smaller interests. What do you think and why?
Do you think people today have enough free time to pursue hobbies? Why or why not?
Imagine a friend who feels bored and unfulfilled. What kind of hobby would you suggest for them and why?
What challenges might someone face when trying to find a hobby they genuinely enjoy?
Some people believe productive hobbies — such as learning a language or playing music — are more valuable than passive ones like watching TV. What do you think?
Has a hobby ever helped you develop a skill that was useful in your work or studies? Tell me about it.
Describe a creative hobby that you think would benefit almost anyone. Why do you think it has broad appeal?
What do you think stops people from continuing a hobby they have already started? How can this be overcome?
Some people argue that people with rich hobbies tend to be more productive at work. Do you agree?
Do you think team-based hobbies are better than solo ones for personal development? Why or why not?
If you had unlimited time and money, what hobby would you pursue and how would you approach it?
What obstacles might an introvert face when choosing or maintaining social hobbies? How could these be addressed?
Some people believe governments should provide more funding and facilities to support citizens' leisure activities. Do you agree?
Is there a traditional hobby or craft from your culture that you think should be preserved? Why?
Describe a hobby that a parent, grandparent, or older relative has. What do you find interesting about it?
What do you think are the psychological benefits of having a creative outlet, such as painting, writing, or music?
Some people argue that competitive gaming should be officially classified as a professional sport. What is your view?
Do you think hobbies become less important as people get older and busier? Why or why not?
Imagine a child with no clear interests. What activities would you introduce to help them discover a hobby?
What do you think is the difference between a hobby and a true passion? Does the distinction matter?
Some people believe schools should allocate regular time for students to explore and develop personal hobbies. Do you agree?
How much time per week do you think a person needs to truly benefit from a hobby? Why?
Describe an outdoor hobby that you think is underrated or underappreciated. Why would you recommend it?
What social or financial barriers might stop someone from pursuing an expensive hobby like sailing or skiing?
Some people argue that digital hobbies — such as gaming or content creation — are just as valid as traditional ones. What do you think?
Has technology changed the way people pursue hobbies? In what ways — positive or negative?
Think of a hobby that naturally connects people across different generations. What makes it capable of bridging age gaps?
What do you think are the risks of turning a personal hobby into a profession or business?
Some people believe that people who lack hobbies are more likely to suffer from stress and low mood. Do you agree?
Do you think the hobbies people choose reflect their personality? Why or why not?
Describe a hobby that could be both enjoyable and environmentally friendly. Why is that combination valuable?
What do you think are the challenges of maintaining a hobby during a very stressful period of your life?
Some people argue that leisure time is becoming a luxury that only wealthier people can afford. Do you agree or disagree?

topicCategories.title: Topic 6 通勤与交通 Commuting & Transport

questions.refText: What kinds of places do you usually travel to during the week, and how do you get there?
Can you describe how someone in your family or a close friend usually travels to work or school? How long does it take and what transport do they use?
Some people believe that living close to work or school greatly improves quality of life, while others think location does not matter much. What do you think and why?
Some people believe employers should reimburse employees for their commuting costs. Others think employees should pay for their own travel. Which view do you agree with and why?
How would you describe the public transport system in your city? Is it convenient and affordable?
Think of someone who has a very long daily commute. How do you think it affects their daily life and well-being?
What do you think are the main reasons people choose to drive to work rather than use public transport?
Some people argue that city centres should be completely car-free to reduce pollution and improve liveability. Do you agree?
Have you or someone you know ever experienced a particularly stressful or unusual journey? What happened?
Describe how a student living in a rural area might travel to school. What challenges might they face compared to a student in the city?
What do you think are the environmental consequences of large numbers of people driving to work every day?
Some people believe public transport should be completely free for all citizens, funded by taxes. What is your view?
Do you think people in your city walk or cycle enough as part of their daily routine? Why or why not?
Think about the difference in daily travel between someone in a city and someone in a rural area. Who do you think has a more convenient lifestyle?
What changes could be made to public transport to encourage more people to use it instead of driving?
Some people argue that self-driving cars will solve most urban traffic problems within the next twenty years. Do you agree?
Do you think commuting time is wasted time, or can it be used productively? Why?
Describe the ideal commute for someone who works full-time. What would make it comfortable and efficient?
What problems does heavy traffic congestion cause beyond inconvenience, and how could they be addressed?
Some people believe that working from home should become the norm to eliminate most commuting. What do you think?
Is cycling a realistic commuting option in your city? What would need to change to make it more viable?
Think about an elderly person who no longer drives. How do you think their daily mobility and independence are affected?
What safety concerns are associated with cycling or walking on busy roads in a city?
Some people argue that high-speed rail should replace domestic flights to reduce carbon emissions. Do you agree?
Do you think the cost of commuting is a significant financial burden for most workers? Why or why not?
What impact do you think poor road infrastructure has on a country's economy and quality of life?
Some people believe that people who drive to work alone should pay a daily congestion charge. What is your view?
How do you think the COVID-19 pandemic changed people's commuting habits, and do you think those changes will last?
Think of a city or country known for its excellent public transport. What do you think makes it so effective?
What barriers prevent more people from cycling or walking to work, even when the distance is manageable?
Some people argue that young people should be given free public transport passes to reduce car dependence early. Do you agree?
Do you think ride-hailing apps have improved or worsened urban transport overall? Why?
Describe the experience of someone who relies entirely on public transport for all their daily needs. What challenges do they face?
What role do you think urban planning plays in how much people need to travel each day?
Some people believe airlines should be heavily taxed to discourage short flights that trains could replace. Do you agree?
Do you think the amount of time people spend commuting will increase or decrease over the next twenty years? Why?
Imagine a friend considering moving closer to their workplace. What factors would you advise them to consider?
What effect do long daily commutes have on families, particularly those with young children?
Some people argue that car ownership should be actively discouraged through high taxes and limited parking. What is your opinion?

topicCategories.title: Topic 7 个人目标 Personal Goals

questions.refText: Think of a time, recent or not, when you had something you wanted to achieve. What was it?
In general, how do you handle difficulties or obstacles that come up while working toward a goal?
In your opinion, how important is goal-setting for personal growth?
Do you think children should be taught to set personal goals from an early age? Why or why not?
What is a personal goal that you or someone you know has been working toward for a long time? What has kept them motivated?
What do you think is the most common reason people fail to achieve their personal goals?
Some people argue that setting very ambitious goals leads to more achievement than setting realistic ones. Do you agree?
Do you think having a clear daily routine helps people reach their long-term goals? Why or why not?
Imagine a friend who keeps setting goals but never follows through. What advice would you give them?
What role do other people — friends, family, or mentors — play in helping someone achieve their goals?
Some people believe happiness comes from achieving goals, while others say it comes from the journey itself. Which view do you agree with?
Have you ever abandoned a goal partway through? What made you give up or change direction?
Describe how someone you know has successfully changed a habit or achieved a personal milestone. What did they do?
What is the difference between a short-term and a long-term goal? Which type do you think is more important?
Some people argue that society puts too much pressure on individuals to be constantly improving and achieving. Do you agree?
Do you think it is possible to achieve a major life goal, such as changing careers, later in life? Why or why not?
Think of a goal that involves helping others. What would motivate you to commit to it long-term?
What personal qualities do you think are most essential for achieving long-term goals?
Some people believe financial success is the most important goal a person can have. Do you agree or disagree?
How does failure affect your motivation to keep pursuing a goal? Do you think failure can be valuable?
Imagine a teenager with no idea what they want in the future. How would you help them begin to set personal goals?
What is the relationship between self-confidence and the ability to set and achieve goals?
Some people argue that life goals should be flexible and regularly revised, not fixed and rigid. What is your view?
Do you think people achieve goals better when they work alone or when they work with others? Why?
Describe a professional or academic goal that someone in your family or social circle recently achieved. What did it take?
What are the negative effects of being so focused on goals that you neglect other areas of your life?
Some people believe goal-setting frameworks such as SMART goals are essential tools for personal development. Do you agree?
Does social media help or hinder people's ability to focus on their personal goals? Why?
Think of a creative goal — such as writing a book or learning an instrument. What would make achieving this particularly rewarding?
What is the impact of comparing your progress to other people's achievements? Is comparison motivating or damaging?
Some people argue that work-related goals should not dominate a person's life at the expense of personal relationships. Do you agree?
How important is accountability when working toward a goal? Do you prefer to be held accountable by others or by yourself?
Describe a health or fitness goal that you think most people would benefit from setting. Why that particular one?
What are the emotional challenges of working toward a long-term goal over many years?
Some people believe ambition is a positive quality, while others see it as selfish or destructive. What is your view?
Do you think the goals people set in their twenties are different from those they set in their forties? How and why?
Think of a time when you or someone you know had to completely redefine a goal because of unexpected circumstances. What happened?
What is the relationship between self-discipline and the ability to achieve goals over time?
Some people argue that pursuing goals is less important than learning to be content with what you already have. Do you agree?

topicCategories.title: Topic 8 消费习惯 Spending Habits

questions.refText: Can you tell me about a recent purchase you or someone in your family made and why you decided to buy it?
When you make a purchase, do you usually plan ahead or do you buy things spontaneously? Why?

Some people believe that creating a monthly or weekly budget is essential for managing finances effectively. Which is more important to you — getting quality for the money or spending less overall? Why?
Do you think people will increasingly rely on financial apps and tools to manage their money, or will they prefer to hire personal financial managers? Explain your thoughts.
Do you think people in your country tend to spend more than they can afford? Why or why not?
Describe the shopping habits of someone in your family or a close friend. Would you say they are a careful or impulsive spender?
What factors do you think most strongly influence whether someone spends money wisely or impulsively?
Some people believe that credit cards encourage people to spend beyond their means and should be more tightly regulated. Do you agree?
Do you tend to compare prices before making a purchase, or do you usually buy the first option you see? Why?
Tell me about a time when you or someone you know made a purchase that turned out to be a very good decision. What made it worth it?
What challenges might someone face when trying to reduce their everyday spending significantly?
Some people argue that advertising is specifically designed to make people buy things they do not need. Do you agree?
Do you think people spend too much money on things that give them temporary happiness, such as food delivery or entertainment? Why?
Describe how someone you know approaches big purchases, such as a new phone or appliance. What process do they go through?
What do you think makes it difficult for young adults to build healthy spending habits when they first start earning money?
Some people believe that schools should teach teenagers about budgeting and financial planning. Do you agree?
Do you prefer to buy high-quality items that last longer, or cheaper items that you replace more often? Why?
Think of a purchase you or someone you know regretted. What led to the decision and what did you learn from it?
What do you think are the biggest challenges people face when trying to stick to a monthly budget?
Some people believe that buy-now-pay-later services are harmful because they encourage people to overspend. What is your view?
Do you think online shopping has changed people's spending habits for better or worse? Why?
Describe how a person you know approaches seasonal sales or discount events. Do they tend to save money or spend more?
What do you think is the relationship between emotional state and impulsive spending? Can you give an example?
Some people argue that luxury goods and designer brands are a waste of money. Do you agree or disagree?
Do you think the cost of living has become harder to manage for most people in your country? Why or why not?
Tell me about a spending habit of yours or someone you know that changed significantly over time. What caused the change?
What do you think are the advantages and disadvantages of using cash versus digital payment methods?
Some people believe that environmental impact should be a key factor when deciding what to buy. Do you agree?
Do you tend to save money for future goals, or do you prefer to spend it on experiences now? Why?
Describe how someone in your family or a friend manages their monthly expenses. What methods do they use?
What do you think are the main reasons young people find it difficult to save money?
Some people argue that companies should be required to make it easier for customers to cancel subscriptions and return products. Do you agree?
Do you think people are more influenced by what their friends spend money on than they realize? Why?
What challenges might a family on a limited income face when trying to cover both essentials and occasional treats?
Some people believe that fast fashion is unethical because it encourages people to buy and throw away cheap clothing constantly. What is your view?
Do you think the way people spend money reflects their values and priorities? Can you give an example?
Tell me about a financial decision — large or small — that someone you know made recently that you thought was wise. Why did it stand out?
What do you think is the biggest challenge for people who want to spend more consciously and ethically?
Some people argue that governments should impose higher taxes on luxury goods to reduce wasteful spending. Do you agree?

topicCategories.title: Topic 9 个人理财 Personal Finance

questions.refText: How often do you review your money management — such as tracking expenses or budgeting? Do you do this weekly, monthly, or less often?
Can you describe one or two methods you or someone you know uses to manage finances? For example, do you use a budgeting app or keep a written record?
Tell me about a financial goal you have achieved recently. What motivated you to achieve it?
Some people believe that financial responsibility education should be mandatory in schools to help students manage money as adults. Do you agree or disagree? Why?
Do you think most people your age have good money management habits? Why or why not?
Describe how a family member or close friend approaches saving money. What strategies do they use?
What do you think are the main reasons people struggle to save money even when they earn enough?
Some people argue that banks and financial institutions do not do enough to help ordinary people manage their money. Do you agree?
Have you or someone you know ever set a savings goal and actually achieved it? What was the experience like?
Describe a method someone you know uses to track or limit their daily spending. How effective is it?
What challenges might a young person face when trying to manage their finances independently for the first time?
Some people believe that investing in stocks or property is too risky for ordinary people and should be avoided. What is your view?
Do you think it is important to have an emergency fund? Why or why not?
Think of a financial decision that someone you know made that turned out to be very wise. What did they do and why was it a good decision?
What do you think are the main obstacles to planning for retirement, particularly for people in their twenties?
Some people argue that financial planning should be taught as a core subject in secondary school alongside maths and science. Do you agree?
Do you think you know enough about managing your personal finances? What would help you learn more?
Can you describe how someone in your family or social circle handled a significant unexpected expense? How did they manage?
What do you think makes it difficult for people to talk openly about money with friends and family?
Some people believe that social media and influencer culture are making people spend more than they can afford. Do you agree?
Do you think it is better to pay off debt as quickly as possible or to invest money at the same time? Why?
What do you think are the financial challenges specific to living in a city compared to a smaller town?
Some people argue that governments should do more to protect people from predatory lending and high-interest credit. Do you agree?
Do you think renting a home is better than buying one in today's economic climate? Why or why not?
Tell me about a financial habit — big or small — that someone you know developed that made a real positive difference. What was it?
What do you think are the advantages and disadvantages of relying heavily on digital payment and mobile banking?
Some people believe that it is irresponsible to spend money on luxuries when you still have debt. Do you agree?
Do you think people are generally more financially responsible in their thirties than in their twenties? Why?
What do you think is the relationship between financial stress and overall health and happiness?
Some people argue that the gender pay gap means women face specific financial challenges that men do not. Do you agree?
How important do you think it is to have clear financial goals at different stages of life? Why?
What do you think are the risks of relying too heavily on credit cards for everyday spending?
Some people believe that cryptocurrency is a legitimate and important part of modern personal finance. What is your view?
Do you think financial stress is one of the most significant sources of anxiety for people today? Why or why not?
Describe a conversation about money that someone you know found helpful or eye-opening. What did they discuss?
What do you think are the challenges of maintaining good financial habits when your income is irregular or unpredictable?
Some people argue that universal basic income would remove financial stress and allow people to live more freely. Do you agree or disagree?

typeCategories.title: Type A · 是非观点题 Yes/No Opinion

questions.refText: Do you think exercising daily is important for good health? Why or why not?
Do you think it is important to have a regular daily routine? Why or why not?
Do you think living in a city is better than living in the countryside? Why?
Do you think cooking at home is better than eating at restaurants? Why or why not?
Do you think it is important to learn a second language? Why?
Do you think people today are more stressed than in the past? Why or why not?
Do you think reading books is still important in the digital age? Why?
Do you think it is important to have a hobby outside of work or school? Why or why not?
Do you think university education is necessary for success in life? Why?
Do you think public transport is better than owning a private car? Why or why not?
Do you think spending time outdoors is important for health? Why?
Do you think technology has made people more or less connected to each other? Why?
Do you think it is important for children to learn how to cook? Why or why not?
Do you think it is better to live near your family or far away? Why?
Do you think keeping pets is good for people's well-being? Why or why not?
Do you think exercise benefits mental health as much as physical health? Why?
Do you think people should plan their careers carefully from a young age? Why or why not?
Do you think it is important to stay in touch with old friends? Why?
Do you think shopping online is better than shopping in stores? Why or why not?
Do you think it is important to volunteer in your community? Why?

typeCategories.title: Type B · 个人经历题 Personal Experience

questions.refText: Do you have a hobby or interest that you regularly spend time doing? Tell me about it.
Did you enjoy your school experience as a child? What made it positive or negative?
Tell me about a school project or activity you participated in. What made it memorable?
Think of a time when you worked on something with other people. What did you learn from that experience?
Think of a time when you faced a challenge and managed to get through it. What happened?
Tell me about a meal or type of food that is special to you. What makes it meaningful?
Do you have a regular exercise routine? Tell me about it.
Think of a trip or journey that was memorable for you. What made it stand out?
Tell me about something you taught yourself to do. How did you go about learning it?
Do you have a daily habit that you think has made a positive difference to your life? Tell me about it.
Think of a time when someone's advice really helped you. What was the situation?
Tell me about a subject or class you enjoyed at school. What made it enjoyable?
Do you have a tradition or regular activity that your family does together? Tell me about it.
Think of a time when you tried something new and it surprised you. What happened?
Tell me about a skill you have that took a long time to develop. How did you build it?
Do you have a favorite place you like to go to relax or think? Tell me about it.
Think of a time when you helped someone. What did you do and how did it make you feel?
Tell me about something creative you have made or done. What was the process like?
Do you have a book, film, or piece of music that has stayed with you? Tell me about it.

typeCategories.title: Type C · 假设建议题 Hypothetical / Recommendation

questions.refText: Imagine you are going to recommend an exercise program for a friend who does not currently exercise. What kind of routine would you suggest and why?
Imagine you are recommending a study method to a friend who struggles to concentrate. What would you suggest and why?
Imagine you are helping a family member who wants to start eating more healthily. What changes would you recommend?
Imagine a friend wants to reduce stress after work. What kind of relaxation routine would you suggest?
Imagine you are recommending a hobby to someone who spends too much time on their phone. What would you suggest?
Imagine a friend wants to improve their English at home. What would you recommend they do every day?
Imagine you are helping a friend who wants to get better sleep. What routine would you suggest?
Imagine a family member wants to get fit but does not want to join a gym. What would you recommend?
Imagine a friend is moving to a new city and wants to meet people. What would you suggest they do?
Imagine you are recommending a travel destination to someone who has never traveled abroad. Where would you suggest and why?
Imagine a friend wants to learn to cook but has never tried before. What would you recommend they start with?
Imagine you are helping a friend who wants to save money but finds it difficult. What practical steps would you suggest?
Imagine a friend wants to start reading more but does not know where to begin. What would you recommend?
Imagine a colleague wants to improve their work-life balance. What changes would you suggest?
Imagine you are helping a child choose an after-school activity. What would you recommend and why?
Imagine a friend wants to feel more energetic during the day. What daily habits would you suggest?
Imagine you are recommending a way for someone to practice a new language with no money. What would you suggest?
Imagine a friend wants to build a closer relationship with their neighbors. What would you recommend?
Imagine a student wants to manage exam stress better. What would you suggest they do?
Imagine a friend wants to eat out less and cook at home more. What practical tips would you give?

typeCategories.title: Type D · 挑战分析题 Challenge & Problem-Solving

questions.refText: What do you think might challenge your friend the most about sticking to a regular exercise program? How would you help them?
What do you think might be the biggest challenge for someone trying to quit smoking? How could they overcome it?
What challenges might a student face when starting university for the first time? How could they cope?
What might prevent someone from starting a new hobby, even if they are interested in it? How could this be addressed?
What do you think is the hardest part of learning a new language? How can learners deal with it?
What challenges might an elderly person face when trying to use a smartphone? How could they be helped?
What might prevent a person from eating healthily when they are very busy? What solutions would you suggest?
What do you think might challenge someone who is trying to work from home productively? How could they manage?
What obstacles might prevent someone from exercising regularly after a long break? How would you help them restart?
What challenges might a parent face when trying to limit their child's screen time? How could they handle it?
What might make it hard for someone to save money consistently? What practical steps would help?
What do you think is the most difficult part of living alone for the first time? How can people adjust?
What challenges might someone face when trying to wake up earlier every day? What strategies would help?
What might prevent someone from maintaining a close friendship when they live in different cities? How could they keep in touch?
What do you think challenges most people when they try to change an unhealthy habit? How would you advise them?
What obstacles might a shy person face when trying to make new friends? How could they overcome shyness?
What might challenge someone who is starting their first job? How could they adapt quickly?
What do you think makes it hard to maintain a good work-life balance? How could someone improve it?
What might prevent someone from following a healthy sleep routine? What would you suggest they change?
What challenges might a person face when trying to reduce their use of social media? How could they stay focused?

typeCategories.title: Type E · 他人描述题 Describing Others

questions.refText: Can you describe how someone in your family or a friend usually travels to work or school? How long does it take and what transport do they use?
Can you describe how someone in your family or a close friend usually travels to work or school?
Tell me about a person you know who has very healthy habits. What do they do?
Can you describe what a typical weekend looks like for someone in your family?
Tell me about a friend or family member who has an interesting or unusual hobby. What do they do?
Can you describe how someone close to you handles stress? What do they usually do?
Tell me about a person you admire. What is it about them that you find impressive?
Can you describe the daily routine of someone in your household?
Tell me about a friend or family member who has recently made a big change in their life. What did they do?
Can you describe how a friend or family member usually spends their free time?
Tell me about someone you know who is very good at saving money. What habits do they have?
Can you describe the eating habits of someone in your family? Would you say they are healthy?
Tell me about a person who helped you learn something important. What did they do?
Can you describe how a friend or family member usually deals with a problem or setback?
Tell me about someone you know who has a very active social life. What do they do?
Can you describe how someone in your family or a friend uses technology in their daily life?
Tell me about a person in your life who is very organized. How do they manage their time?
Can you describe how a friend or classmate approaches their studies? What methods do they use?
Tell me about a family member who has a skill or talent you admire. How did they develop it?
Can you describe how someone you know stays motivated when things get difficult?

typeCategories.title: Type F · 立场评价题 Opinion on a Viewpoint

questions.refText: Some people believe that it is important to have some sort of reward after a good week of exercising. Do you agree or disagree? Why?
Some people believe that social media does more harm than good. Do you agree or disagree? Why?
Some people argue that it is more important to enjoy your work than to earn a high salary. Do you agree?
Some people believe that governments should ban all advertising of junk food. Do you agree?
Some people argue that working from home is more productive than working in an office. Do you agree?
Some people believe that zoos should be abolished because keeping animals in cages is wrong. What do you think?
Some people argue that university should be free for all students. Do you agree or disagree?
Some people believe that it is better to have one deep friendship than many casual ones. Do you agree?
Some people argue that competitive sports put too much pressure on children. Do you agree?
Some people believe that parents are more responsible for teaching children values than schools are. What do you think?
Some people argue that technology has made people less creative. Do you agree or disagree?
Some people believe that public transport should be free for all citizens. Do you agree?
Some people argue that people who make unhealthy lifestyle choices should pay more for healthcare. Do you agree?
Some people believe that the ability to listen is more important in a leader than the ability to speak. What do you think?
Some people argue that taking a year off between school and university is a waste of time. Do you agree?
Some people believe that it is better to specialize in one skill than to have many general abilities. What is your view?
Some people argue that the news media should be required to remain completely neutral. Do you agree?
Some people believe that young people today are less independent than previous generations. Do you agree?
Some people argue that physical education should be compulsory throughout all years of school. Do you agree?
Some people believe that learning a musical instrument should be required in primary school. What do you think?
